realmDetails.description: The cycle of birth, death, and rebirth driven by karma

keyMyths.title: The Wheel of Life (Bhava Chakra)
keyMyths.description: The Bhava Chakra is a symbolic representation of Samsara, depicting the six realms of existence (gods, demigods, humans, animals, hungry ghosts, and hell beings) and the causes of suffering. Held by Yama, the Lord of Death, it illustrates the cyclical nature of rebirth driven by ignorance, attachment, and aversion.
keyMyths.source: Abhidharma texts, such as the Abhidharmakośa

keyMyths.title: Mount Meru Cosmology
keyMyths.description: Mount Meru is the central mountain in Buddhist cosmology, surrounded by continents, oceans, and other mountains. It's the axis mundi, representing the connection between the different realms of existence and serving as the abode of various deities and celestial beings.
keyMyths.source: Sūtra literature, particularly the Aggañña Sutta and cosmologies described within the Vinaya

keyMyths.title: The Story of the Buddha's Enlightenment
keyMyths.description: Siddhartha Gautama's journey to enlightenment under the Bodhi tree is central. Mara, the embodiment of delusion and death, tempts him with worldly pleasures and attacks him with armies of demons, but Siddhartha remains steadfast and attains enlightenment, becoming the Buddha.
keyMyths.source: Buddhavamsa, Jataka Tales, and various sutras detailing the life of the Buddha

keyMyths.title: The Jataka Tales
keyMyths.description: The Jataka tales are a collection of stories about the previous lives of the Buddha, in both human and animal forms. These stories illustrate the virtues (paramitas) that the Buddha cultivated over countless lifetimes, such as generosity, compassion, and wisdom, leading to his eventual enlightenment.

keyMyths.title: The Pure Lands (Sukhavati)
keyMyths.description: In Pure Land Buddhism, Sukhavati is a celestial realm created by Amitabha Buddha, a place of perfect bliss and conducive to spiritual practice. Rebirth in Sukhavati is attained through faith in Amitabha and recitation of his name, offering a path to liberation outside the cycle of Samsara.
keyMyths.source: Larger Sutra of Immeasurable Life, Smaller Sutra of Immeasurable Life, and Contemplation Sutra

sources.name: The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism
sources.description: An authoritative and comprehensive reference work on Buddhist terminology, concepts, and history.
sources.title: The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism

sources.name: Early Buddhist Texts
sources.description: Collection of suttas and vinaya texts representing the earliest stratum of Buddhist teachings.
sources.title: Early Buddhist Texts

sources.name: The Lotus Sutra
sources.description: A highly influential Mahayana Buddhist scripture emphasizing the potential for universal Buddhahood.
sources.title: The Lotus Sutra

sources.name: The Abhidharmakośa
sources.description: A comprehensive compendium of Buddhist Abhidharma philosophy by Vasubandhu.
sources.title: The Abhidharmakośa

sources.name: The Tibetan Book of the Dead (Bardo Thodol)
sources.description: A guide for navigating the intermediate state (bardo) between death and rebirth, offering instructions for liberation.
sources.title: The Tibetan Book of the Dead (Bardo Thodol)
